gaps: No test covers the DFU success screen UI content verification — specifically checking whether the correct component name (e.g., 'Charge Stand') is displayed vs. an incorrect name (e.g., headset name) after a single-component firmware update.
No test covers the scenario of updating only the Charge Stand (or a single non-headset component) via Device Settings > Software, leaving the headset un-updated.
No test validates the success screen text/string accuracy after a partial DFU (updating only one component of a multi-component device like Voyager Free 60+).
No test covers post-DFU UI verification for component-specific naming in success dialogs — a known pattern where the wrong device name could be displayed due to shared success screen logic.

generated_testcases.title: TC-DFU-SUCCESS-001: Verify Charge Stand name on DFU success screen after updating only the Charge Stand
steps: 1. Launch Poly Studio Mobile app and connect to Poly Voyager Free 60+ UC.
2. Navigate to Device Settings → Software (or Device → [Device Name] → Software).
3. Verify the Software screen shows two components: 'Headset' and 'Charge Stand' with their respective firmware versions.
4. If a firmware update is available for the Charge Stand only, proceed. Otherwise, ensure the headset is on the latest firmware and only the Charge Stand has a pending update.
5. Tap 'Update' or 'Update All' — note: if the UI offers per-component update, tap 'Update' next to 'Charge Stand' only.
6. Wait for the Charge Stand firmware update to complete (do NOT interrupt the process).
7. Observe the success/completion screen that appears after the update finishes.
8. Verify the success screen text reads: 'Charge Stand updated successfully' or similar wording that explicitly names 'Charge Stand' (not 'Headset' or the device name 'Voyager Free 60+ UC').
9. Verify the success screen does NOT display the headset name (e.g., 'Voyager Free 60+ UC' or 'Headset') as the updated component.
generated_testcases.expected: The DFU success screen displays the correct component name 'Charge Stand' (not the headset name or the overall device name). The text explicitly indicates the Charge Stand was updated.

generated_testcases.title: TC-DFU-SUCCESS-002: Verify headset name on DFU success screen after updating only the Headset
steps: 1. Launch Poly Studio Mobile app and connect to Poly Voyager Free 60+ UC.
2. Navigate to Device Settings → Software.
3. Verify the Software screen shows 'Headset' and 'Charge Stand' components.
4. Ensure only the Headset has a pending firmware update (Charge Stand is up-to-date).
5. Tap 'Update' next to 'Headset' (or 'Update All' if per-component update is not available, but confirm only headset updates).
6. Wait for the headset firmware update to complete.
7. Observe the success/completion screen.
8. Verify the success screen text reads: 'Headset updated successfully' or similar wording that explicitly names 'Headset'.
9. Verify the success screen does NOT display 'Charge Stand' or the overall device name as the updated component.
generated_testcases.expected: The DFU success screen displays the correct component name 'Headset' (not 'Charge Stand' or the overall device name).

generated_testcases.title: TC-DFU-SUCCESS-003: Verify success screen after updating both Headset and Charge Stand simultaneously
steps: 1. Launch Poly Studio Mobile app and connect to Poly Voyager Free 60+ UC.
2. Navigate to Device Settings → Software.
3. Verify both 'Headset' and 'Charge Stand' have pending firmware updates.
4. Tap 'Update All' to update both components.
5. Wait for both firmware updates to complete sequentially.
6. Observe the final success/completion screen after both updates finish.
7. Verify the success screen text mentions both components: e.g., 'Headset and Charge Stand updated successfully' or lists both names.
8. Verify neither component name is missing or replaced by the overall device name.
generated_testcases.expected: The DFU success screen correctly displays both component names ('Headset' and 'Charge Stand') when both were updated.

generated_testcases.title: TC-DFU-SUCCESS-004: Verify success screen after Charge Stand update — app force-kill and relaunch
steps: 1. Launch Poly Studio Mobile app and connect to Poly Voyager Free 60+ UC.
2. Navigate to Device Settings → Software.
3. Ensure only the Charge Stand has a pending firmware update.
4. Tap 'Update' next to 'Charge Stand' to start the update.
5. After the update completes and the success screen appears, force-kill the app (swipe it away from the recent apps list).
6. Relaunch Poly Studio Mobile app.
7. Reconnect to the Voyager Free 60+ UC device.
8. Navigate to Device Settings → Software.
9. Verify the Software screen shows the updated firmware version for the Charge Stand.
10. Verify the success screen does NOT reappear (it should be a one-time dialog).
generated_testcases.expected: The DFU success screen is shown once after update completion and does not reappear after app force-kill and relaunch. The firmware version correctly reflects the Charge Stand update.

generated_testcases.title: TC-DFU-SUCCESS-005: Verify success screen text after Charge Stand update — device disconnect/reconnect during success screen
steps: 1. Launch Poly Studio Mobile app and connect to Poly Voyager Free 60+ UC.
2. Navigate to Device Settings → Software.
3. Ensure only the Charge Stand has a pending firmware update.
4. Tap 'Update' next to 'Charge Stand' to start the update.
5. Wait for the update to complete and the success screen to appear.
6. While the success screen is displayed, turn off the Voyager Free 60+ UC earbuds (place them in the case and close the lid, or power off via the touchscreen case).
7. Wait 5 seconds, then turn the device back on and allow reconnection.
8. Observe the success screen after reconnection.
9. Verify the success screen still displays 'Charge Stand updated successfully' (or equivalent) and does not change to an incorrect name.
10. Dismiss the success screen and verify the Software screen shows the updated firmware version for the Charge Stand.
generated_testcases.expected: The DFU success screen persists correctly with 'Charge Stand' name even after a device disconnect/reconnect event during the success screen display.

generated_testcases.title: TC-DFU-SUCCESS-006: Verify success screen after Charge Stand update — navigate away and return
steps: 1. Launch Poly Studio Mobile app and connect to Poly Voyager Free 60+ UC.
2. Navigate to Device Settings → Software.
3. Ensure only the Charge Stand has a pending firmware update.
4. Tap 'Update' next to 'Charge Stand' to start the update.
5. Wait for the update to complete and the success screen to appear.
6. Without dismissing the success screen, tap the back button or navigate to the Home screen of the app.
7. Navigate back to Device Settings → Software.
8. Verify the success screen is no longer displayed (it should have been dismissed on navigation).
9. Verify the Software screen shows the updated firmware version for the Charge Stand.
10. Verify the headset firmware version remains unchanged.
generated_testcases.expected: Navigating away from the success screen dismisses it. The Software screen correctly reflects the Charge Stand firmware update while the headset version is unchanged.

generated_testcases.title: TC-DFU-SUCCESS-007: Verify success screen shows correct name when Charge Stand update fails and then succeeds on retry
steps: 1. Launch Poly Studio Mobile app and connect to Poly Voyager Free 60+ UC.
2. Navigate to Device Settings → Software.
3. Ensure only the Charge Stand has a pending firmware update.
4. Tap 'Update' next to 'Charge Stand' to start the update.
5. During the update, simulate a failure (e.g., move the device out of Bluetooth range, or turn off the charge case).
6. Observe the failure/error screen — verify it mentions 'Charge Stand' (e.g., 'Charge Stand update failed').
7. Bring the device back into range / power on the charge case.
8. Tap 'Retry' or 'Update' again for the Charge Stand.
9. Wait for the update to complete successfully.
10. Observe the success screen — verify it reads 'Charge Stand updated successfully' (not 'Headset' or the device name).
generated_testcases.expected: Both the failure screen and the subsequent success screen correctly reference 'Charge Stand' as the component being updated.
